title: planchonella dubia
intro: Planchonella dubia, also known as the black plum or black sapote, is a tropical fruit-bearing tree native to the Pacific Islands. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Planchonella dubia include its ability to support cardiovascular health, lower blood pressure, and reduce the risk of certain cancers due to its high content of antioxidants and flavonoids. In herbal preparations, Planchonella dubia is often made into jams, jellies, and teas, as well as being used in traditional remedies for fever, cough, and digestive issues.
